votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks that the lengths of the lists are equal and that the output list is the element-wise absolute difference of the two input lists, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it checks that game and guess have equal length, computes the expected output as the list of absolute differences of paired elements, and returns True iff out equals this expected list. This matches the Coq Prop which asserts the same conditions.
